598.reason: [After the main loop ends, if the last character read is a single '&' or '|', the current logic checks isAnd() or isOr() using curchar, but curchar may not correspond to the last token's trailing single '&'/ '|' because getchar() may have advanced beyond it or the file ended; more critically, this check uses the global curchar which might be from a new line or invalid state, leading to missed error detection or incorrect error reporting. Additionally, the condition should check whether the last unfinished token was a single '&' or '|', not the current curchar.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [After the main loop ends, if the last character read is a single '&' or '|', the current logic checks isAnd() or isOr() using curchar, but curchar may not correspond to the last token's trailing single '&'/ '|' because getchar() may have advanced beyond it or the file ended; more critically, this check uses the global curchar which might be from a new line or invalid state, leading to missed error detection or incorrect error reporting. Additionally, the condition should check whether the last unfinished token was a single '&' or '|', not the current curchar.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition checks the current global `curchar` after full tokenization, but at this point `curchar` may not correspond to the dangling '&' or '|' that caused the error; moreover, the logic fails to handle the case where a single '&' or '|' appears at the very end of file—because `getsymbol()` already returned -1 and the final token (which is just "&" or "|") was never processed by the `isAnd()`/`isOr()` branches in `getsymbol()`. Thus, this late check is both logically incorrect and ineffective.]
